data.title: Adeia stock falls on insider share sale by chief legal officer
data.description: Investing.com - Adeia Inc (NASDAQ:ADEA) shares dropped 6.6% in premarket trading Monday after the company’s Chief Legal Officer Kevin Tanji sold a significant portion of his holdings.Tanji sold 99,342 shares of common stock on May 13 at a weighted average price of $31.75, according to a Form 4 filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The shares were sold in multiple transactions at prices ranging from $31.34 to $32.15. Following the sale, Tanji retained 312,913 shares of the company.The transaction represents a notable insider sale at the technology company, which specializes in intellectual property licensing and media solutions. Insider sales can sometimes signal concerns about a company’s near-term prospects, though executives often sell shares for personal financial planning reasons unrelated to company performance.The filing did not provide a specific reason for the stock sale. Adeia has not issued any public statement regarding the transaction.This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

data.title: Sterling today: Pound steadies as U.S.-Iran sanction waiver talk lifts mood
data.description:
Investing.com - Sterling found tentative footing on Monday after last week’s bruising selloff, though the recovery masks a currency still under considerable strain from a toxic mix of domestic political uncertainty and a worsening global inflation shock driven by the ongoing US-Iran conflict.
As of 08:43 ET (12:43 GMT), GBP/USD rose 0.52% to 1.3394, while EUR/USD nudged 0.22% higher to 1.1651.
The pound’s inability to mount a more convincing recovery reflects lingering anxiety over the domestic political landscape. Investors continue to monitor the potential entry of Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham into Westminster via a Makerfield by-election, potentially scheduled for mid-June to early July.
[[pro_promotion |
Get real-time market-moving headlines and analyst alerts on InvestingPro 55% off
]]
The pound’s inability to mount a more convincing recovery reflects lingering anxiety over the domestic political landscape. Investors continue to monitor the potential entry of Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham into Westminster via a Makerfield by-election, potentially scheduled for mid-June to early July.
Markets regard Burnham as the least fiscally orthodox candidate to inherit the Labour leadership. Deutsche Bank noted that Labour’s National Executive Committee is now expected to clear his path to Westminster, keeping the tail risk alive.
Goldman Sachs flagged that prediction markets had already been pricing a leadership transition by year-end for some time, attributing sterling’s recent underperformance less to the prospect of change itself and more to a narrowing in the timeline, compounded by macro factors already pressuring the UK fiscal outlook.
The brokerage noted EUR/GBP has moved from trading broadly in line with cyclical fundamentals to incorporating around 1 percentage point of fiscal premium, still light relative to the roughly 2% premium that prevailed for much of last year, which Goldman sees as a reasonable near-term target pending greater policy clarity.
A global bond rout deepened on Monday as rising energy prices fanned inflation fears. Benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yields jumped to 4.6310%, with the two-year yield touching 4.1020%, both near their highest levels since February 2025.
Markets are now pricing in a greater than 50% probability that the Federal Reserve will raise rates by December, according to the CME FedWatch tool, lending the dollar broad support and leaving sterling with little room to recover.
Crude prices continue to grind toward four-year highs, with stalled U.S.-Iran negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz showing no signs of resolution.
U.S. President Donald Trump’s renewed frustration with Tehran has deepened fears the closure could prove prolonged, sustaining upward pressure on energy costs globally.
For the UK, heavily dependent on energy imports, the damage is acute. Markets now price in nearly three Bank of England rate hikes this year, with Chief Economist Huw Pill reinforcing the case for further tightening last week, arguing the energy-driven inflation shock demands a firm policy response.
Until the Makerfield by-election resolves political uncertainty and the Strait of Hormuz reopens, sterling’s recovery is likely to remain fragile.

data.title: Starmer rejects departure timetable amid Labour leadership crisis
data.description:
Investing.com - British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will not provide a timeline for leaving office, his spokesman said Monday, as pressure intensifies for him to step aside amid a leadership crisis within the Labour Party.
"There will be no timetable for departure," Tom Wells, Starmer's official spokesman, told reporters Monday. "The prime minister is getting on with governing."
[[pro_promotion |Stay ahead of every breaking move with real-time news on InvestingPro — save 50% now. ]]
Starmer's position has weakened after almost 100 lawmakers called for his resignation following poor results in local elections this month. Energy Secretary Ed Miliband is among senior ministers who have urged him to outline a departure plan, but Starmer has declined.
"Keir Starmer remains the most resilient person I know in my life," Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy said on Sky News Monday. "We now need to unite and pull together."
During a visit to Labour Party headquarters in London Monday, Starmer told staff he remains focused on his duties.
"I am focused on the job that I was asked to do, which is to serve my country and to carry out my duties as prime minister of this country," he said, according to a transcript from a Labour Party spokesperson. "Delivering for the very many people who voted us into office, who are saying, 'just get on with it, get on with the job, get on with the change that I need to see in my life.'"
The internal party revolt, which included the resignation of Health Secretary Wes Streeting, has created conditions resembling a leadership contest, though no candidate has secured the required support of 81 Members of Parliament to trigger a formal ballot.
Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham is the current front-runner and is expected to be selected to contest a by-election in Makerfield next month. A Westminster seat is required for Burnham to challenge Starmer. Streeting has also indicated he will join any leadership contest.
Starmer said Monday he would support "one hundred percent" whichever candidate Labour selects for Makerfield.
"I want every member, everyone in our movement to support them," Starmer said. "A Labour candidate to beat Reform. That is the fight that we are in."

data.title: Iran launches Bitcoin insurance for Hormuz Strait shipping
data.description:
Investing.com - Iran has launched a Bitcoin-backed insurance service for shipping companies transiting the Strait of Hormuz, the semi-official Fars news agency reported Monday, citing documents from Iran's Ministry of Economy and Financial Affairs.
The service, named Hormuz Safe, could generate more than $10 billion in revenue for Iran, according to Fars, though no timeframe or operational breakdown was provided.
[[pro_promotion |Stay ahead of every breaking move with real-time news on InvestingPro — save 50% now.]]
Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for global energy supplies, since US and Israeli airstrikes began on Feb. 28. The government and Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps have been working to formalize control over the waterway through tolls and fees.
According to a screenshot of the Hormuz Safe website shared by Fars, the service targets Iranian shipping companies and cargo owners. Iran's use of cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Tether has increased since President Donald Trump targeted its economy and energy exports during his first administration.
Cryptographically verifiable insurance policies will cover shipments passing through the Persian Gulf, the Strait of Hormuz and surrounding waterways, with payments settled in Bitcoin, Fars reported, citing the website hormuzsafe.ir. Coverage begins upon confirmation, with signed receipts provided to owners.
The US and Iran have maintained a "shaky" ceasefire since early April.
Trump threatened Iran on Sunday, warning Tehran to "better get moving, FAST, or there won't be anything left of them" in a Truth Social post. Both sides have rejected each other's demands and cannot agree on restarting peace talks.
More than 1,500 commercial vessels were trapped in the Persian Gulf as of early May, according to the US military. Regional oil producers have been forced to cut output as storage capacity runs out.
Iran and the IRGC have allowed some vessels to transit via a designated route near Iran's coast, sometimes after requesting payments up to $2 million. The US has blockaded Iranian ports in response.
The connection between Hormuz Safe and the IRGC or government toll plans remains unclear. Fars is affiliated with the Guards.
Ebrahim Azizi, head of Iran's parliamentary commission for national security, said a professional mechanism to manage strait traffic along a designated route would be unveiled soon. Only commercial vessels and parties cooperating with Iran would benefit, Azizi said in a statement on X. Fees will be collected for specialized services, and parties involved in the US-Israeli war on Iran would be banned from the route.

data.title: Quantum BioPharma stock surges on MS imaging study progress
data.description: Investing.com - Quantum BioPharma Ltd. (NASDAQ:QNTM) shares rose 10% in premarket trading Monday following the company’s announcement that patient enrollment in its collaborative study with Massachusetts General Hospital reached the halfway mark with encouraging preliminary imaging results.The study is designed to validate a positron emission tomography (PET) imaging technique capable of directly assessing demyelinated neurons with intact axons and tracking demyelination in patients with multiple sclerosis. The research utilizes the PET tracer [¹⁸F]3F4AP, developed by Pedro Brugarolas, an investigator in the Department of Radiology at MGH and Assistant Professor at Harvard Medical School.The first cohort of participants has been successfully imaged using both advanced PET/MR and total-body PET platforms. Preliminary analyses indicate robust signal in acute MS lesions and potential sensitivity to gray matter lesions, according to the company."We are excited to reach this important midpoint in our study with MGH and encouraged by the strength of the preliminary imaging data," said Dr. Andrzej Chruscinski, Vice-President, Scientific and Clinical Affairs at Quantum BioPharma.The imaging approach could enable more precise measurement of how therapies impact myelin preservation and regeneration, potentially enhancing the evaluation of treatments including Quantum’s investigational candidate, Lucid-MS (Lucid-21-302).Lucid-MS is designed to provide neuroprotection through the inhibition of demyelination by targeting protein arginine deiminase 2 (PAD2), an enzyme implicated in myelin degradation. The company submitted an IND for Phase 2 approval to the FDA in March 2026.In prior Phase 1 clinical trials, Lucid-MS demonstrated a favorable safety profile and was well-tolerated in healthy participants.This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

data.title: Buy this China chip stock, Citi says
data.description:
(Updated - May 18, 2026 8:20 AM EDT)
Citi upgraded a major Chinese semiconductor company to Buy on Monday, citing accelerating domestic chip localization, rising AI-related demand, and improving pricing power as the company heads into a stronger second quarter.The bank upgraded SMIC, China’s largest chipmaker, after the company reported first-quarter revenue of $2.5 billion, up 12% year-on-year and 1% sequentially, with gross margin improving to 20.1% and utilization rates remaining high at 93.1%.While the result beat Citi’s own estimates, it came in slightly below broader consensus expectations.“The company outperformed normal seasonality as domestic substitution, recovering consumer electronics demand, and stronger industrial and automotive demand supported growth,” Citi analysts said.Profit attributable to shareholders came in at $197.4 million for the quarter, also missing the consensus estimate.China’s share of SMIC’s revenue rose to 88.9%, pointing to an "accelerating localization trend,” the bank noted.The company guided second-quarter revenue growth of 14–16% sequentially, with gross margin of 20–22%, both ahead of market expectations. The outlook is supported by improving average selling prices (ASPs), a better product mix, and stronger order visibility."AI-related demand is increasing," Citi noted, adding that SMIC is also seeing upside in power management chips, specialty memory, display driver ICs, and automotive chips.The company said it has grown more “optimistic” about its overall operations this year compared to the December quarter, citing robust customer demand and existing orders.Mature nodes above 28nm remain the core profit driver, though SMIC continues to advance its 14nm and selective 7nm technology.At the same time, capital expenditure moderated sequentially, which Citi viewed positively as a sign of more disciplined expansion and improving cash flow quality, while SMIC maintains investment in specialty and mature-node capacity."Given improving UTR, pricing and solid demand, we upgrade SMIC to Buy," the analysts wrote.As China’s most advanced chip foundry, SMIC sits at the center of Beijing’s drive for technological self-sufficiency, a push made more urgent by U.S. restrictions on access to advanced semiconductors used in artificial intelligence. That policy backdrop has positioned the company to benefit as Chinese firms increasingly turn to domestic suppliers.Still, the company faces headwinds, including the ongoing memory supply crunch and broader geopolitical tensions. Those concerns contributed to a nearly 30% decline in SMIC’s shares in the first quarter, as investors also fretted over the costs and complexity of scaling advanced chip capacity.

data.title: History suggests S&P 500 drops at least 7% when this happens
data.description:
Investing.com - One technical signal in the S&P 500 is flashing warning signs for stocks, with historical data pointing to a possible significant pullback ahead, according to BTIG.
The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE:SPY) on Thursday registered a daily Relative Strength Index (RSI) reading of 78, a level indicating overbought conditions. The following day, SPY fell 1.2%.
[[pro_promotion |
Get premium market insights on InvestingPro, save up to 50%
]]
BTIG technical strategist Jonathan Krinsky notes that since 2023, there have been only six instances where the SPY ETF has lost more than 1% after the RSI exceeded 75. RSI is a momentum indicator that measures how overbought or oversold a security is on a scale of 0 to 100, with readings above 70 generally considered overbought.
In five of those six instances, SPY suffered a peak-to-trough decline of at least 7% in the weeks that followed, Krinsky said. The lone exception, in 2023, saw the market trade sideways before resuming its uptrend. Average returns were negative across every timeframe from 5 to 40 days.
The RSI signal arrived alongside a broader deterioration in market internals that Krinsky said had been building for weeks. Friday’s session brought the first meaningful crack in the semiconductor and AI trade, with the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ:SMH) posting its worst single-day performance since March, falling 3.8%.
Despite some rotation into software names, breadth was broadly weak, with 70% of NYSE volume concentrated in declining stocks, the highest such reading since March. 
"This is consistent with our view that semis/AI are likely to ‘catch down’ to the rest of the market as opposed to the other way around," Krinsky said. 
Adding to the concerns is an unprecedented divergence in index breadth. The S&P 500 on Thursday sat 8.5% above its 50-day moving average while only 47% of its member stocks traded above their own 50-day averages, marking "by far the widest divergence on record," according to Krinsky. 
Furthermore, the 30-year Treasury yield has climbed back above 5% and is threatening to break above its 2023 cycle peak of around 5.17%. While the S&P 500 index itself has largely shrugged off rising rates, the average stock has not, with 9% of S&P 500 members hitting 52-week lows as recently as last Wednesday.
Krinsky warned that a sustained move to a higher trading range for long-dated yields "would likely finally be a headwind for even the tech/AI names that have remained immune thus far."
Tech concentration is another reason for caution, as the S&P 500 technology sector’s weighting has surged to a fresh record of 37.5%, surpassing its peak during the dot-com bubble.

data.title: Global bonds under pressure as oil prices resume advance
data.description:
Investing.com - Eurozone bond yields moved higher on Monday, echoing a sell-off in global government debt, as investors fretted over the impact of an energy shock on inflation and interest rates.
[[pro_promotion |
Track Eurozone bond yields with InvestingPro
]]
The yield on the Germany 10-year bond, a benchmark for the Eurozone, rose to its highest level in 15 years. Yields in France, Italy and Spain all climbed as well.
In the U.S., the yield on the benchmark 10-year note also touched a 15-month high, and yields in Japan hit peaks not recorded since 1996.
Oil prices once again marched higher against this backdrop, with Brent crude futures hovering above $110 a barrel. Prior to the start of the war in late February, Brent was exchanging hands at around $70 a barrel. 
Over the weekend, a drone strike hit a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia said it had intercepted three drones. U.S. President Donald Trump also said Iran must act "fast" to secure a long-term peace deal, testing a fragile ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.
Meanwhile, the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway off of Iran’s southern coast through which a fifth of the world’s oil transits, remains effectively closed to tanker traffic, as it has for much of the conflict.
Fears have swirled around whether a prolonged energy shock could lead to an inflation burst that causes central banks to lift interest rates. Government bond yields, which tend to move inversely to prices, have shot higher as a result.
Along with exerting pressure on households and governments through higher borrowing costs, elevated bond yields can also raise the discount for future company earnings, possibly denting stock valuations.
"The sell-off in bond markets is the dominant story in global financial markets," analysts at ING said in a note.

data.title: Wall Street futures tick up as yields stabilize, oil prices fall
data.description:
Investing.com - U.S. stock index futures ticked higher on Monday, as a global bond sell-off eased and yields stabilized. Still, the topic of inflation and tighter monetary policy to combat it remained in the spotlight.
Investors were also keeping an eye out for any breakthrough in a continued impasse between the U.S. and Iran. This week will also see quarterly earnings from artificial intelligence heavyweight Nvidia.
At 08:54 ET (12:54 GMT), S&P 500 futures were up 0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures climbed 0.6%, and Dow futures added 0.1%. 
[[pro_promotion |Get premium insights on Wall Street moves with InvestingPro
]]
Nvidia, Walmart results in focus for Wall St cues
On Friday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.1%, while the S&P 500 dropped 1.2% and the NASDAQ Composite lost roughly 1.5% as investors reacted to rising bond yields and oil prices.
Despite the selloff, the S&P 500 still notched its seventh straight weekly gain, while the Nasdaq also finished the week higher. Both benchmarks had hit record highs last week.
Treasury yields remained elevated after hotter-than-expected U.S. inflation data reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve may keep interest rates higher for longer.
Investors this week will focus squarely on Nvidia’s earnings on Wednesday for clues on the durability of the artificial intelligence-driven rally that has propelled megacap technology shares and broader equity indexes higher this year.
Retail giant Walmart (NASDAQ:WMT) is due to report on Thursday, with traders watching for signs that elevated fuel costs are beginning to pressure consumer spending.
Trump says ’clock is ticking’ for Iran; oil prices climb
Geopolitical tensions also remained firmly in focus after fresh security incidents in the Gulf.
The United Arab Emirates said a drone strike triggered a fire near the Barakah nuclear power plant. Saudi Arabia separately said it intercepted three drones that entered from Iraqi airspace.
“For Iran, the Clock is Ticking, and they better get moving, FAST, or there won’t be anything left of them. TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE!” Trump wrote on Truth Social on Sunday.
Axios reported that Trump is scheduled to meet top national security advisers on Tuesday to discuss possible military options regarding Iran, citing two U.S. officials.
The comments added to fears of a broader escalation in the Middle East as the Strait of Hormuz remained effectively closed.
Oil prices continued to rise on Monday, with Brent rising above $110 per barrel, fuelling concerns that higher energy costs could worsen inflation pressures and weigh on consumer spending.
Markets were also digesting the outcome of last week’s closely watched summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing. The two leaders sought to stabilize ties strained by trade disputes, but did not announce any major deals.
Ayushman Ojha contributed to this article
